title: Hymenochlaena pteroclada
taxonomy: Hymenochlaena pteroclada is classified in the family Restionaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as restios. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its narrow, grass-like leaves and its growth habit. Restionaceae is a family of monocotyledonous plants, which means that their seeds have only one cotyledon or seed leaf. The family Restionaceae is part of the order Poales, which includes other families of flowering plants such as the grasses and the sedges. The classification of Hymenochlaena pteroclada reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
benefits: Hymenochlaena pteroclada is a lesser-known plant species, but research on its potential health benefits is emerging.
